title: Death and EoE Timeline Question
wikitext:
[]: Death & EoE Timeline Question             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 03, 2006 2:02 pm I have a continuity-wise question. With those Quartet scenes in Death, it's not supposed be within the time-line as the series, right? Not only did it say some weird line (according to the fansub I saw) about that scene happening "18 months eariler" or something like that (weird because Kaworu wasn't around then, nor were Shinji (at his Uncle's or teacher's, whoever the hell it was) and Asuka (In Germany (I think??) or America (Supposedly her nationailty's American, despite being German-Japanese), or wherever it was she was living with Kaji), but the Refrain of Evangelion booklet said something about that Bach Cello piece being "played by a boy who look(s)/ed just like Shinji." :shock: My guess is that it's supposed be showing yet another possible world? For instace, when Asuka waltzs in, she says "Good morning, Ikari!" instead of calling him Shinji, and seems to be more or less nice to him. One more question, off-topic to this but it relates to movies. What exactly are the purpose of those A10 Connector things? Is it just to connect the pilot's nerve impluses/etc to the Eva? Because I don't believe Shinji was wearing that in EoE (kinda hard for him to run and get it with everyone's dying all around him and the floor he was just on exploded, anyway (Why Misato?! :cry:) ), so is it not required to pilot?? Also, End of Eva happened around January 1st, 2016, right? (What a way to start off the New Year! :roll:) Because according to 25, "the year is 2016", and if they can be seen as being concurrent, then... (Wiki is a very happy thing. Not only would I have not known this, but I also wouldn't have known how the hell Kaworu appears! (Rei absorbes Gendou's hand, which has Adam, and Adam=Kaworu (soul-wise, at least!)) Ugh. My brain hurts just from trying to figure this out! *sigh* It's times like this I wish I had that storyboard info like Reichu does. @ Reichu-sama: That's what I was trying to ask you about how you know/have all that info, I just couldn't think of what it was called. ^^;

[]: Re: Death & EoE timeline question             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 03, 2006 2:04 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I have a continuity-wise question. With those Quartet scenes in Death, it's not supposed be within the time-line as the series, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 03, 2006 2:09 pm That's part of the reason I why I started wondering about all that...

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Tue Oct 03, 2006 3:00 pm The first time I saw it was with a fansub, and it said 18 months later. It screwed my concept and expectations of End of Evangelion.

[]: Re: Death & EoE timeline question             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 03, 2006 6:02 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Nope, Asuka was comatose before Kaworu even appeared.

[]: Re: Death & EoE timeline question             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Tue Oct 03, 2006 8:58 pm Reichu wrote: IBUKI: Affirmative! (to headset) Stop medication to pilot. Prepare for launch!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 03, 2006 10:05 pm Why would she just slip into a coma for a couple of days after being confiscated from a dirty bathtub, then conveniently slip out of it shortly after being removed from her hospital bed?

[]: Re: Death & EoE timeline question             Quote Post by cat42 » Tue Oct 03, 2006 11:06 pm Reichu wrote: Asuka's father ("Papa" Langley) is American, and Kyoko is German/Japan. The American citizenship would imply that Asuka was born there.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 03, 2006 11:34 pm I really don't know how it works, but Asuka's American citizenship is a recurring tidbit in NGE sources. It doesn't seem to ever come up in the show, so I'm not sure what the point of this background detail would be. No mention is made of German citizenship, even though this is the country where she has evidently lived most of her life. Just the country where Daddy came from. I'm pretty sure that you're an American citizen if you're born there -- and Langley and Kyoko had to meet and reproduce sometime, somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Oct 04, 2006 1:36 am No they didn't, the hell-spawn attachted herself upon those poor people, driving Kyoko insane! Asuka was hatched from the red egg of discontentment, sent to Earth to suck the life engergy out of people (not like that you perverts). *Runs before he is lynched by Asuka fans, fails, and is found hanging from a tree the next morning*

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Oct 04, 2006 6:52 am Reichu wrote: Why would she just slip into a coma for a couple of days after being confiscated from a dirty bathtub, then conveniently slip out of it shortly after being removed from her hospital bed?

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Oct 04, 2006 7:23 am cat42 wrote: No they didn't, the hell-spawn attachted herself upon those poor people, driving Kyoko insane! Asuka was hatched from the red egg of discontentment, sent to Earth to suck the life engergy out of people (not like that you perverts). *Runs before he is lynched by Asuka fans, fails, and is found hanging from a tree the next morning*

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 04, 2006 8:38 am jcmoorehead wrote: as regards to Asukas citizenship I think she's a German citizen

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Wed Oct 04, 2006 10:41 am Reichu wrote: jcmoorehead wrote: as regards to Asukas citizenship I think she's a German citizen

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 04, 2006 10:54 am Classified Information wrote: 3. The Second Children A. Publicly Released Information Asuka Langley Soryu is the pilot of Eva Unit 02. Although her nationality is American, she is 1/4 German and 1/4 Japanese. She graduated from a German university at the age of fourteen.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 04, 2006 1:00 pm She is certainly sufficiently ethnic-German to immediately qualify for German citizenship (IIRC, one German great-grandparent would suffice).

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Oct 04, 2006 9:22 pm Yes, but the Reichstag decrees don't apply in Eva. The other day I read a guy who claimed Asuka's full name was, officially, Souryuu Asuka Langley Zeppelin. So when I asked what his source was, he quoted german law. External reference which doesn't apply. No external reference says how Eva is.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Thu Oct 05, 2006 12:35 am OT, but does the topic really need "[SPOILERISH]" in it? Anyone who ventures into the "Evangelion Discussion" forum without having seen it all deserves to have the show ruined for them. Actually, I take it back -- no one deserves that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 11, 2006 6:38 pm So, back to my other question, what about the continuity? Are the scenes in Death just meant to take place in "another possible world"? Side-topic, but... is S2 Works avalible to buy anywhere or is that OOP? (Oh well, if so, there's always the Internet! :mrgreen:)

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Oct 11, 2006 7:44 pm I'm gonna go with alternate universe.

For instace, when Asuka waltzs in, she says "Good morning, Ikari!" instead of calling him Shinji, and seems to be more or less nice to him. One more question, off-topic to this but it relates to movies. What exactly are the purpose of those A10 Connector things? Is it just to connect the pilot's nerve impluses/etc to the Eva? Because I don't believe Shinji was wearing that in EoE (kinda hard for him to run and get it with everyone's dying all around him and the floor he was just on exploded, anyway (Why Misato?! :cry:) ), so is it not required to pilot?? Also, End of Eva happened around January 1st, 2016, right? (What a way to start off the New Year! :roll:) Because according to 25, "the year is 2016", and if they can be seen as being concurrent, then... (Wiki is a very happy thing. Not only would I have not known this, but I also wouldn't have known how the hell Kaworu appears! (Rei absorbes Gendou's hand, which has Adam, and Adam=Kaworu (soul-wise, at least!)) Ugh.
